response: The Bahá'í Faith has several important collections of writings that are central to its teachings. One significant compilation is the ["Compilation of Compilations"](https://siftersearch.com/library/Baha'i/Baha'i%20Books/youness-afroukhteh_memories-of-nine-years-in-akka#p1836), which was put together by the Research Department of the Universal House of Justice. Additionally, the writings of Bahá'u'lláh, such as the [*Tablet to Mirza Abu'l-Fadl*](https://siftersearch.com/library/Baha'i/Tablet%20Translations/bahaullah_tablet-to-mirza-abul-fadl-concerning-the-questions-of-manakji-limji-hataria#p39), are foundational to the faith, offering guidance on spiritual and practical matters.
